str_content:
CASE REFERENCE: STR-2026-04-04-5428

REPORTING ENTITY: Union Bank of India, AML Compliance Division

SUBJECT ACCOUNTS: 
ACC05200000000012 - Vikram Patel - Suspicious total: ₹ 8,33,124.00 (calculated from key transactions)

FRAUD PATTERN IDENTIFIED: 
The account holder, Vikram Patel, has been identified to be involved in a structuring/smurfing pattern. Multiple transactions have been made below the reporting threshold of ₹ 10 lakhs to avoid reporting requirements. The transactions also show a deviation from normal patterns, indicating a potential money laundering activity.

TRANSACTION TIMELINE: 
• 2025-09-11 19:16:00 - ₹ 99,226.67 - NEFT - ACC05200000000014 - Pooja Agarwal
• 2025-09-11 19:24:00 - ₹ 89,801.67 - NEFT - ACC05200000000027 - Sanjay Dubey
• 2025-09-11 19:36:00 - ₹ 86,672.46 - NEFT - ACC05200000000023 - Mohan Lal
• 2025-09-11 19:48:00 - ₹ 87,589.23 - NEFT - ACC05200000000019 - Radha Krishnan
• 2025-09-11 20:05:00 - ₹ 85,554.73 - NEFT - ACC05200000000005 - Rajesh Yadav
• 2025-09-11 20:24:00 - ₹ 91,301.17 - NEFT - ACC05200000000037 - Rekha Pandey 4
• 2025-09-11 20:38:00 - ₹ 96,403.21 - NEFT - ACC05200000000024 - Anita Desai
• 2025-09-11 20:54:00 - ₹ 93,790.03 - NEFT - ACC05200000000039 - Harish Joshi 3
• 2025-09-11 21:16:00 - ₹ 86,676.86 - NEFT - ACC05200000000038 - Pooja Agarwal 7

REGULATORY BASIS: 
The transactions reported are in contravention of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002 (PMLA), specifically sections 3 and 4. The RBI AML Master Circular also requires banks to report suspicious transactions to the Financial Intelligence Unit - India (FIU-IND). The reported transactions are also in contravention of RBI AML Master Circular clauses 3.1 and 3.2.

RECOMMENDED ACTION: 
Account Freeze. The account ACC05200000000012 should be frozen immediately to prevent further suspicious transactions. The Financial Intelligence Unit - India (FIU-IND) should be notified through the goAML portal. The Enforcement Directorate (ED) should also be referred to for further investigation. Enhanced monitoring of the account and related parties should be implemented.

RISK RATING: HIGH 
The risk rating is high due to the large number of suspicious transactions and the potential involvement in money laundering activities.

INVESTIGATING OFFICER: 
[To be assigned]
